chunks.text: Graduate Student Homepages | EECS at UC Berkeley. Graduate Student Homepages. The students' general research areas are given. For more detailed information, see the individual student's homepage.
chunks.question: What is the purpose of the graduate student homepages page?
chunks.answer: To list students and their research areas.

chunks.text: A. Lakshya A Agrawal - Artificial Intelligence. Arwa AlAnqary - Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics. B. Rod Bayliss - Power and Energy. Alex Beaudin - Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics. Vinamra Benara - Artificial Intelligence. Landon Butler - Information, Data, Network, and Communication Sciences.
chunks.question: Which students are listed under the letter A and B?
chunks.answer: Lakshya Agrawal, Arwa AlAnqary, Rod Bayliss, Alex Beaudin, Vinamra Benara, Landon Butler.

chunks.text: C. Catherine Chen - Biosystems & Computational Biology. D. Sampada Deglurkar - Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics. Devon Ding - Theory. Kris Shengjun Dong - Computer Architecture & Engineering. Chris Douglas - Database Management Systems. Ramit Dutta - Physical Electronics.
chunks.question: What research areas are covered under letters C and D?
chunks.answer: Biosystems & Computational Biology, Control, Intelligent Systems, Robotics, Theory, Computer Architecture, Database Management, Physical Electronics.

chunks.text: F. Rachel Freedman - Artificial Intelligence. Zhe Fu - Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics. G. Dekel Galor - Biosystems & Computational Biology. Francesca Giardine - Power and Energy. Adwait Godbole - Programming Systems. Sam Gunn - Theory.
chunks.question: Who are the students in the F and G sections?
chunks.answer: Rachel Freedman, Zhe Fu, Dekel Galor, Francesca Giardine, Adwait Godbole, Sam Gunn.

chunks.text: H. Jae Hong - Operating Systems & Networking. Yixiao Huang - Artificial Intelligence. I. Christian Ikeokwu - Artificial Intelligence. K. Justin Kang - Information, Data, Network, and Communication Sciences. Darya Kaviani - Security. Chanwut Kittivorawong - Human-Computer Interaction.
chunks.question: What are the research areas for students H through K?
chunks.answer: Operating Systems, Networking, Artificial Intelligence, Information Sciences, Security, Human-Computer Interaction.

chunks.text: L. Canal Li - Artificial Intelligence. Tingle Li - Artificial Intelligence. Jiachen Lian - Signal Processing. Long Lian - Signal Processing. Xintian Liu - Micro/Nano Electro Mechanical Systems. Grace Luo - Artificial Intelligence.
chunks.question: Which students are listed under the letter L?
chunks.answer: Canal Li, Tingle Li, Jiachen Lian, Long Lian, Shaokai Lin, Toru Lin, Yu-Chi Lin, Xiaoyuan Liu, Xintian Liu, Grace Luo.

chunks.text: M. David McElroy - Operating Systems & Networking. Hila Mor - Human-Computer Interaction. P. Melissa Pan - Artificial Intelligence. Angelos Pelecanos - Theory. Julien Piet - Security. Sara Pohland - Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics.
chunks.question: What are the names and research areas for students M and P?
chunks.answer: David McElroy - Operating Systems, Hila Mor - Human-Computer Interaction, Melissa Pan - AI, Angelos Pelecanos - Theory, Julien Piet - Security, Sara Pohland - Control.

chunks.text: R. Eric Rawn - Human-Computer Interaction. Bhaskar Roberts - Theory. S. Sheng Shen - Artificial Intelligence. T. Neerja Thakkar - Graphics. Chien-Ting Tung - Physical Electronics. V. Alan Zhu - Artificial Intelligence.

chunks.answer: Eric Rawn - Human-Computer Interaction, Bhaskar Roberts - Theory, Sheng Shen - AI, Neerja Thakkar - Graphics, Chien-Ting Tung - Physical Electronics, Francisca Vasconcelos - AI, David Wu - AI, Haoru Xue - Control, Kevin Zheng - Integrated Circuits, Alan Zhu - AI.
